description:
First implementation phase of bl-f021. Lands the XDG layout as the *only* layout `bl init` writes, and teaches `Store::discover` to read both layouts so existing pre-XDG and bl-ed32 clones keep working unchanged. This is the safety net every later phase rests on — once dual-read ships, the migrate command (Phase 2) becomes a discrete, separately-shippable step.

## Scope

Per SPEC-clone-layout.md §3, §5, §6, §7 (read path) and §11 (the *read side* of migration — applying field renames in memory when reading old files, *without* committing the migration).

### Implements

1. **XDG path materialization in `Store::discover`** (§7 steps 1–8).
   - `<origin-key>` derivation from canonicalized `origin.url` (§4); golden vectors per conformance test §14.2.
   - `<path-hash>` derivation from `git rev-parse --absolute-git-dir` (§4).
   - The three XDG trees under `~/.config/balls/<origin-key>/`, `~/.local/state/balls/<origin-key>/`, `~/.cache/balls/<origin-key>/`.
   - `state-repos/own/` checkout of `origin balls/tasks`.
   - `active/` symlink view (solo and federated branches per §3).
   - `tracker.json` redirect detection + single-hop check (§5, §14.5).
   - Per-clone tree under `<path-hash>/{claims,locks,plugins-auth,worktrees}/`.

2. **Three-file config readers** (§6.1, §6.2, §6.3, §6.4, §6.6).
   - `tracker.json` strict schema (only `state_url`/`state_branch`); §14.6.
   - `repo.json` lenient-unknown reader (§6.9).
   - `project.json` lenient-unknown reader.
   - `specific-wins` precedence merge (§6.6); §14.9.
   - Project-only fields in `repo.json` aborts the read (§6.6); §14.10.

3. **`bl init` writes XDG exclusively.**
   - No `.balls/` at the clone root, no `.gitignore` entries, no `balls: initialize` commit on main.
   - Pushes a `balls/tasks` orphan branch with default `repo.json` + `project.json`, no `tracker.json` (solo case).
   - §14.1, §14.19 (no on-main writes in steady state).

4. **Dual-read for legacy layouts** (safety net).
   - **Pre-XDG layout detection** — `.balls/config.json` on main + `.balls/state-repo/` runtime + `.balls-worktrees/` in tree. When present, read from the in-repo layout instead of XDG; warn one line `legacy layout in use; run \`bl migrate\` to relocate` per §12.
   - **bl-ed32 layout detection** — `state-repos/own/.balls/workspace.json` present (with or without `state_url`). When found, read under the two-file schema; warn similarly. The field-name renames (`delivery`→`integrate`, `review.pre_check`→`review.gate_command`) and the `target_branch` drop are applied **in memory** on the way out of the reader, not written back.
   - Preference rule: if both the new and the old layout exist on the same clone, prefer new (the migrate command in Phase 2 may run mid-way and leave the old around briefly; Phase 1 must already favour the new).

5. **Worktree relocation for new claims** (§8, §14.14).
   - `bl claim` invokes `git worktree add` against `~/.local/state/balls/<origin-key>/<path-hash>/worktrees/<id>/`. On a pre-XDG-detected clone, the legacy in-tree path is preserved (until Phase 2 migrate moves them).

### Explicitly NOT in scope

- `bl migrate` command itself — Phase 2.
- `bl prime --migrate` / auto-detect warning beyond the one-line legacy warning — Phase 3.
- `min_bl_version` bump on the orphan tracker — Phase 4.
- Cross-doc rename of SPEC-forge-gated-delivery §5 (the `delivery`/`target_branch` references in *that* SPEC) — lands with Phase 2's migrate work since they're already conceptually paired.
- `bl doctor` enrichment for moved-clone detection (§14.15) — defer to a follow-up; Phase 1 just needs to not regress doctor's current behaviour.

## Conformance tests gated by this ball

SPEC-clone-layout §14 tests: 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 20, 24, plus §14.16/§14.17 *under the Phase-1 dual-read branch* (read old field names, warn, apply rename in memory). §14.19 (no on-main writes) gated on freshly-initialized clones; existing clones with legacy `.balls/config.json` on main don't satisfy this until Phase 2 runs.

Each test must exist and fail for the right reason against pre-implementation code before the ball lands. New-side assertions per SPEC-tracker-state §16.13 — no pinned old-binary fixtures.

## Why this ball, not three smaller ones

The XDG materialization, three-file readers, and dual-read all bind into the same `Store::discover` function. Splitting them produces intermediate states where (a) discover knows the new paths but no file reader understands the new schema, or (b) the schema readers exist but no path code calls them. Both intermediate states require dead code on main and synthetic test fixtures that don't reflect any real lifecycle. Bundling lets the ball ship a coherent slice — "new clones use XDG, old clones still work."

## Risks

- `Store::discover` is hot — every `bl` invocation runs it. Phase 1's branch-on-layout logic must be O(1) on the common path; the legacy-detection should short-circuit via a single stat of `state-repos/own/` existence.
- Symlink layout under `~/.config/balls/<origin-key>/active/` is six relative-path links — easy to mis-target. Conformance test §14.12 (idempotent re-materialization) is the gate against drift.
- Two clones of the same origin sharing `<origin-key>` means a fetch on either touches a shared `state-repos/own/` working tree. Git's own `index.lock` serializes; conformance test §14.3 asserts the per-clone isolation under contention.
title: Phase 1: dual-read XDG layout, bl init writes XDG only
